title: Bucket Types, Endpoints, Regions, and S3 Express · Runtime Recall
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs bucket types, endpoints, regions, and s3 express from browser, Go service, S3, CloudFront, security, billing, and stakeholder evidence, then challenges one retained misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for bucket-type and endpoint compatibility matrix after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare general-purpose and directory buckets by namespace, endpoint, zone, ordering, throughput, durability scope, versioning, lifecycle, and feature support.
Construct regional virtual-hosted and zonal endpoints without confusing region, availability zone, bucket name, TLS name, or path style.
Resolve intended region and endpoint explicitly and reject redirects, signing-region drift, and accidental cross-region transfer.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Bucket Naming Creation Gate
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Go file-delivery team handles case 5412 during a regional dependency incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate general or directory bucket region, zone, endpoint, reactivation, and session rules; preserve accessible progress, cancellation, status, and recovery evidence; then cancel halfway through and prove every body, part, and goroutine reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Validate globally or zonally scoped naming, ownership, location, public-block, encryption, versioning, tags, and rollback before bucket creation.

Inspect the Bucket Naming Creation Gate trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Bucket Naming Creation Gate.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for validate globally or zonally scoped naming, ownership, location, public-block, encryption, versioning, tags, and rollback before bucket creation. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Bucket Naming Creation Gate, keep Bucket Naming Creation Gate connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Validate globally or zonally scoped naming, ownership, location, public-block, encryption, versioning, tags, and rollback before bucket creation.

For S3, CloudFront, and Go Separate evidence from confidence · Review Bucket Naming Creation Gate, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: Creating a bucket is a harmless runtime operation that needs no reviewed infrastructure gate.

Build deterministic Go evidence to validate general or directory bucket region, zone, endpoint, reactivation, and session rules; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then introduce a duplicate event or ambiguous response and reconcile without duplicate effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare general-purpose and directory buckets by namespace, endpoint, zone, ordering, throughput, durability scope, versioning, lifecycle, and feature support.

For S3, CloudFront, and Go Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review General Directory Bucket, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: Directory buckets are faster general-purpose buckets with identical APIs and guarantees.

options.text: Declare S3, CloudFront, and Go stakeholder outcome, file and object identity, owner, state, and byte, time, concurrency, cache, and cost budgets for the review General Directory Bucket case
Predict S3, CloudFront, and Go authorization, custody, transfer, integrity, cache, failure, cancellation, cleanup, rollback, and recovery outcomes for the review General Directory Bucket case
Run the smallest deterministic S3, CloudFront, and Go storage decision model and retain inspectable changed-case evidence for the review General Directory Bucket case
Inject one changed S3, CloudFront, and Go failure, verify bounded cleanup and recovery, and state controlled AWS transfer limits for the review General Directory Bucket case

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Bucket Naming Creation Gate
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Bucket Naming Creation Gate: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Go file-delivery team handles case 1983 during an SDK and infrastructure canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate general or directory bucket region, zone, endpoint, reactivation, and session rules; record cleanup, rollback, restoration, and residual-risk ownership; then double concurrent transfers and keep process resource limits intact. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Validate globally or zonally scoped naming, ownership, location, public-block, encryption, versioning, tags, and rollback before bucket creation.

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Bucket Naming Creation Gate.
steps.why: Retrieving Bucket Naming Creation Gate through the concrete bucket-type and endpoint compatibility matrix case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Record what required effort · Review Bucket Naming Creation Gate, keep Bucket Naming Creation Gate connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Validate globally or zonally scoped naming, ownership, location, public-block, encryption, versioning, tags, and rollback before bucket creation.
